_note: Pilot crop manifest for replacing full-page rendered figures with cropped figure-only images. Coordinates are pixel boxes [left, top, right, bottom] measured against source_size (the source page render's actual pixel dimensions). box_type is always 'pixel' for now; a 'normalized' box_type may be added later if renders are regenerated at different resolutions.
_status: pilot - only 4 entries populated (ch01, Figures 1-1 through 1-4); do not batch-generate the rest until this workflow is reviewed
_rules: Figure-only crop images must be tightly framed around the figure content (photo/diagram), not the surrounding page whitespace.
Avoid large margins: crop boxes should be derived from the actual content bounding box on the source render, not eyeballed page-fraction estimates.

Multi-part figures that appear as sub-images sharing one physical figure/caption on the source page (e.g. two side-by-side photos under a single caption like 'Figure 1-7 and Figure 1-8') should be cropped as one combined figure block, unless the source page clearly presents them as separate, independently-labeled figures.

Every cropped figure referenced from Markdown must be visually checked (via Read/image preview) for correct size, framing, and presence before being considered done - do not assume a crop is correct from coordinates alone.

crops.notes: Photo of security bit selection, top of page 034 render. Tightened from the pilot v1 box, which clipped the top of the tallest bit and left excess right/bottom margin.
Top photo on page 041 render (Xbox bottom case, screw locations). Confirmed OK in pilot review; left unchanged.

Bottom photo block on page 041 render: two side-by-side sub-photos (hand prying rubber foot / hand pressing foot back) sharing a single 'Figure 1-3' caption. Cropped as one combined figure block per the multi-part-figure rule. Was missing from the pilot v1 manifest/Markdown; added here.
Photo of screws hidden by serial number / certification labels, page 042 render. Tightened from the pilot v1 box, which clipped the left edge and left excess right margin.
